name: Garlic Mashed Potatoes
recipeIngredient: 4 Large russet potatoes, peeled and quartered
1 Cup goat cheese at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Season with salt and white pepper to taste.
2. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
3. Wrap the unpeeled garlic cloves in a piece of aluminum foil. Drizzle with olive or canola oil to prevent sticking during cooking.
4. Bake the wrapped garlic in the preheated oven for 350 degrees until soft, about 45 minutes to 1 hour.
5. Meanwhile, bring water to a boil in a pot on the stove. Cook the potatoes in boiling water until fork tender, about 20 to 30 minutes (see note).
6. Once cooked, cool the garlic cloves and gently squeeze out the soft garlic from its outer skin into a bowl. The garlic should have a pasty texture.
7. Drain the potatoes and return them to the pot. Crumble the goat cheese into the potatoes and add the cooled roasted garlic paste.
8. Add 1/4 cup of potato cooking liquid to the mixture. Mash together by hand for best texture, or use a hand mixer. Continue mashing until all ingredients are well combined but slightly lumpy.
9. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and white pepper as needed.
10. Serve immediately. Makes 8 servings.
Note: Steaming potatoes instead will retain more vitamin C, which dissolves into the cooking water. Per serving: 140 calories, 21g carbohydrate, 5g protein, 4g total fat (2.3g saturated fat), 11mg cholesterol, 1.5g fiber, 79mg sodium, 53mg calcium, and 8 mg Vitamin C. Calories from fat constitute 26%.
